item.title: For Spacecoin to Truly Differentiate Itself, it Must Move Beyond Corporate Decision-making Models
item.author: Edwin Liava'a
item.content:encoded: As I've explored in my recent articles about satellite technology and blockchain integration, we stand at a crucial juncture in the evolution of global connectivity. While Spacecoin's vision of combining satellite networks with blockchain technology shows immense promise, its true potential lies in embracing the fundamental principle that made Bitcoin standout from the rest, absolute decentralization.\ The core strength of Bitcoin isn't just its technology, it's the governance model that Satoshi Nakamoto established. By remaining truly decentralized, without any controlling entity, Bitcoin has achieved something remarkable, a system that belongs to everyone and no one simultaneously. This is the model that Spacecoin should emulate if it wants to create a truly revolutionary satellite-based internet infrastructure.\ My experience at Kacific Broadband Satellites Group, where we explored the concept of "Space Nodes," highlighted a crucial truth but technical innovation alone isn't enough. The governance structure determines whether a system serves its users or becomes another form of centralized control. While our Space Nodes project was technically sound, it remained within traditional corporate structures. Spacecoin has the opportunity to break free from these constraints.\ For Spacecoin to truly differentiate itself, it must move beyond corporate decision-making models. Rather than having decisions flow from a centralized authority, the network should evolve through consensus of its participants. Network upgrades would emerge not from boardroom decisions, but from the collective will of the community. Changes would be implemented only when broad consensus exists, ensuring that the network truly serves its users rather than corporate interests.\ The Pacific region's recent experiences with natural disasters and infrastructure failures have shown us the vulnerabilities of centralized systems. When undersea cables fail or traditional infrastructure crumbles, communities are left disconnected and vulnerable. A truly decentralized Spacecoin network could provide not just reliable connectivity, but ensure that control over this vital infrastructure remains in the hands of its users. The network's resilience would come not just from its satellite technology, but from its distributed governance structure that prevents any single point of failure or control.\ As we watch Starlink deploy incredible technological advances, like their V3 satellites with 1 Tbps downlink speeds, we must remember that technical capability and governance are distinct concerns. While Starlink proves what's technically possible, Spacecoin has the opportunity to prove what's democratically achievable. Through transparent, public decision-making processes and equal voting power based on network participation, Spacecoin could create a system naturally resistant to corporate or governmental control.\ The future of global connectivity shouldn't just be faster or more resilient, it should be freer. By following Bitcoin's governance model, Spacecoin could create something unprecedented, a satellite network that's not just technically decentralized, but democratically unstoppable. Each participant in the network would have a voice in its evolution, ensuring that the system remains true to its founding principles of openness and accessibility.\ The journey toward true decentralization isn't easy, but it's necessary. As someone who has witnessed multiple "roads not taken" in the blockchain space, I believe this is one road Spacecoin must travel if it wants to create lasting change in global communications infrastructure. The promise of satellite-based internet isn't just about reaching new heights technologically, it's about reaching new heights in how we govern our digital infrastructure. Let Spacecoin be the project that finally proves that true decentralization isn't just possible, but essential for the future of global connectivity.

item.title: This 7-Step Framework Guided Me to $300K in Monthly Revenue
item.author: James Effarah
item.content:encoded: We’ve just hit £30K per month in revenue for a product launched only 10 months ago. If we were to start over today, here’s the roadmap we’d follow to secure those first 10 customers and then methodically power up to the next 100.Step 1: The Friends + Family Round – Building Your Initial Customer BaseLaunching a product can feel as thrilling—and occasionally as nerve-wracking—as setting foot into a wilderness. But one of the simplest ways to navigate early challenges is to lean on your existing network. This often-overlooked approach can be transformative when carried out with intention.Identify Your Target CompaniesDefine Your Ideal Customer Profile (ICP): First, pinpoint exactly whom you intend to serve. Think of it like selecting the ideal habitat for a species you’re studying: consider the industry, geographic reach, and size of companies that might be most receptive to your product’s benefits.Compile a List of 100 Potential Companies: Use platforms such as Crunchbase and LinkedIn to identify organizations that match your ICP criteria. Aim for a broad mix—both fledgling startups and established enterprises—to keep your potential “ecosystem” diverse and fertile for new relationships.Research and Identify Key Contacts For every company in your crosshairs, look for four or five individuals who could champion or influence the adoption of your product. Think of them as keystone species that can dramatically shape the environment—people in roles like product management, marketing, or operations.Analyze Employee Profiles: Review their professional histories to glean shared interests or mutual connections. Just as ecologists rely on clues in the environment, look for hints such as common groups or mutual acquaintances to make your outreach feel personal and relevant.Utilize Mutual Connections Use LinkedIn’s “Connections” feature to trace any mutual contacts. In nature, symbiotic relationships can be surprisingly powerful—think of this as harnessing existing bonds to foster new ones. Draft a concise, genuine request for your shared contact. Highlight how your product might be beneficial, but remember to keep it friendly and respectful of boundaries. As any field researcher might tell you, sometimes you have to venture beyond your comfort zone. Politely ask for assistance, staying both humble and transparent about your intentions.Craft Personalized OutreachDevelop a Value Proposition: When you reach out, speak directly to the specific issues or opportunities you uncovered. Similar to a scientist presenting relevant data, ensure your pitch resonates with your contact’s role, challenges, or interests. The objective is to kindle a relationship, not to throw a hard pitch on the first encounter. Ask questions, gather feedback, and refine your product as you learn from each interaction.Maintain Consistent Communication: After your first contact, reinforce the conversation with useful information, relatable anecdotes, or case studies that speak directly to their needs. Everyone loves an early reward. Give prospective customers special pricing or features to motivate their commitment to your product.Gather Feedback and Iterate Like a diligent researcher, probe for candid thoughts on what works and what doesn’t. Let this new data set guide your next steps. Be ready to pivot. If your initial approach sparks lukewarm reactions, your willingness to adapt can be the difference between failure and flourishing.Build a Strong Foundation A single successful encounter is not the end—it’s a seed. Keep watering it with follow-ups, tips, and resources. Eventually, it can grow into powerful testimonials. Authentic praise from these early supporters can act like a beacon in the wild, attracting more like-minded prospects into your orbit.Step 2: The Ask: Emphasize Advice and FeedbackWhen you reach out, avoid leading with, “Can I sell you something?” Instead, invite your potential contacts to share their expertise. A well-framed query might be:“We’re launching something in this space. I know you’ve encountered a lot of solutions in your field. Could I bounce a few ideas off you for next year’s product strategy?”\ Interestingly, in about one out of five cases, the person will respond with, “Let’s talk about how we might use this.” When they broach the subject first, it shifts the conversation dynamic and creates a more receptive environment for genuine collaboration. Remember, out of 100 such calls, only 8 may be with the right person—but the entire 100 will guide you in identifying who that right person might be.Step 3: Develop a LinkedIn Content StrategyLinkedIn can be a fertile watering hole for finding and engaging the professionals who align with your offering. Much like an ecologist attempting to attract specific pollinators, it’s crucial to understand your audience’s habits and craft the right signals.\ Define Your Target AudienceBefore you post a single update, construct a detailed persona of your ideal customer. Picture their demographic profile, job titles, and the issues they grapple with. For example, you might zero in on a “VP of Content at a 1,000-person DTC brand” who must juggle strategy, analytics, and team leadership.\ Plan Your Content CalendarMaintain consistency by scheduling three LinkedIn posts per week. This regular tempo ensures your audience knows exactly when to expect fresh insights. Tools such as Google Calendar or Trello can be your daily field guides.Keep a note-taking tool at the ready for whenever inspiration strikes. If a colleague mentions a problem mid-meeting or you stumble upon an illuminating case study, jot it down. Over time, you’ll gather a thriving repository of ideas to share with your connections. Open with a surprising statistic or a pithy question. Curiosity is a powerful magnet. Offer clear takeaways, actionable tips, or fresh perspectives backed by data and stories.Include a Call-to-Action (CTA): Prompt readers to comment, share, or visit a relevant resource. An impactful chart or a succinct infographic can crystallize complex ideas in a snap.\ Engage With Your Network:Dialogue is the lifeblood of LinkedIn. When someone reacts to your post, respond thoughtfully. Share others’ content, and join discussions in your feed. Genuine engagement can set off a ripple effect, expanding your influence beyond your immediate circle.Pay close attention to metrics like likes, shares, comments, and click-throughs. Like any good scientist reviewing experimental data, use these insights to refine what resonates best with your audience.Steadily expand your circle by sending personalized connection requests to individuals in your field. A quick note explaining why you find their work intriguing often increases acceptance rates—and sets a warmer tone for future conversations.\ Overcome the Initial Hurdles:At first, your posts may not garner massive engagement. Just as in field research, perseverance is crucial. Each post is a test, a chance to observe patterns and learn from feedback. Keep at it, refine your approach, and soon enough, you’ll find your stride.Building Connections on LinkedIn: A Step-by-Step GuideConstructing a robust LinkedIn network is akin to nurturing a micro-ecosystem: each meaningful connection can either flourish into mutual growth or remain a dormant link. Below is a systematic approach for adding prospects to your network in a way that feels authentic and enduring.\ \ Aim to pinpoint 25 new prospects weekly—be they potential clients, collaborators, or thought leaders in your domain. LinkedIn’s search and Groups features can help you home in on the precise profiles you need.\ Navigate to Their Profile: Visit their page to understand their background and interests. Trigger the connection request from their main profile.Skip the Message (for Now): Although it’s tempting to introduce yourself right away, data suggest that a quiet connection request often yields higher acceptance rates.\ Understand Acceptance RatesTypically, only about 20% of your requests will translate into connections. Out of the 25 you send each week, expect five to be accepted. Don’t be discouraged; rejection or silence is a normal part of the networking environment.\ Avoid Immediate MessagingOnce a prospect accepts your request, resist the urge to message them straight away. Give the relationship room to breathe before diving deeper.When new connections join your network, they’ll see your posts in their feed—your chance to stand out. Whether it’s a thought-provoking article or a concise video snippet, present insights that enhance their day. Develop a sustainable posting rhythm that puts you on their radar without overwhelming them. A like, comment, or thoughtful question on their posts can organically deepen your connection.Over time, consistent visibility and engagement can transform casual connections into genuine professional alliances. Each reply is a nutrient that helps your network grow stronger.Personalize Future Messages: Let your connections see you’ve taken an interest in their opinions or content they shared.Creating Effective Watering Hole PostsImagine a communal resource—a place where peers and industry professionals naturally gather to share knowledge. Watering hole posts fulfill that role on digital platforms like LinkedIn.Use surveys, social media analytics, or direct conversations to learn about your audience’s priorities and obstacles. If you cater to the tech realm, your audience might be eager to discuss artificial intelligence or cybersecurity.Address a Controversial Issue: Spark debate on a hot-button subject in your sector—like wind vs. solar in renewable energy. Forecast how an emerging technology or trend might reshape the market in five years.Highlight a Common Challenge: Invite peers to help solve a notorious bottleneck, such as high employee turnover in tech. An intriguing question or striking fact can act like a sudden rustle in the undergrowth, seizing attention. Draw from personal anecdotes or reliable data to lend credibility and depth. Pose open-ended questions or invite alternative viewpoints to stimulate conversation. Spread the word across multiple channels, using relevant hashtags and tagging influencers where appropriate. Thoughtful and timely replies show your audience you genuinely care about their perspectives.Collaborate With Influencers: Seek out industry luminaries who might share your post to amplify your reach. Track how many likes, shares, and comments your post garners. Gauge how many engaged readers represent viable business or partnership opportunities. Monitor how many conversations, inquiries, or deals stem directly from your post.Step 7: Initiate Outreach and Foster EngagementFinally, let’s talk about reaching out to the new contacts in your LinkedIn circle. The goal here is not to fire off an aggressive sales pitch, but rather to extend an invitation for a deeper dialogue—one that could evolve into a lasting connection.Reach out to individuals who connected with you about a month ago or showed interest in your posts. This spacing allows the contact to feel organic rather than forced.\ Personalize Your ApproachTailor each message to the person’s background, referencing any mutual interests or industry overlaps you’ve observed. This sense of familiarity often warms them to further discussion.\ Propose a test run of your product or an exclusive look at a feature in development. Framing it as a chance for them to offer feedback positions them as co-creators, not just targets.\ I noticed your recent engagement with my post on ==[specific topic]==, and I really appreciated your insights.\ We’re testing out a new feature that aligns with your expertise in ==[relevant field]==.\ I’d love for you to try it and share your feedback, as it will help us refine the final version.\ Let me know if you’re interested, and I can set up a brief trial.\ Emphasize Their InfluenceThink of your connection as a valued collaborator who can shape your product’s trajectory. Highlight how their commentary will impact final decisions or future directions.Encourage them to share experiences or challenges they’re facing. These candid moments often uncover hidden insights that can inspire product improvements.\ Maintain Regular EngagementKeep them in the loop about significant milestones—especially those directly influenced by their suggestions. When people realize they’ve shaped your product, they’re more inclined to champion it.\ Build Long-Term RelationshipsOver time, some of these contacts may become dedicated customers or trustworthy evangelists who amplify your reach by sharing success stories or referrals.Collect data on response rates, feedback quality, and conversion metrics. Continuously refine your strategy based on what resonates. The insights you gather now lay the groundwork for each subsequent outreach cycle.By weaving together personal connections, consistent content strategies, and value-driven engagement, you can replicate a structure that has guided us to $300K per month—and possibly beyond. Like any carefully orchestrated ecological system, success lies in understanding the dynamics at play, nurturing the right relationships, and adapting swiftly to new data.

item.title: Previously Undisclosed Flaw Let Hackers Hijack Millions of Systems Through Innocent-Looking Files
item.author: Fikri Azhar

item.title: Businesses Are Ditching Big GenAI Overhauls for Smarter, Low-Risk Upgrades
item.author: James Effarah
item.content:encoded: Businesses are pivoting from massive overhauls to smaller, strategic applications of GenAI. This new approach emphasizes enhancements over replacements. Instead of dismantling entire systems, companies are embedding GenAI into existing frameworks. The goal? To make operational processes smarter, more efficient, and innovative without risking instability.\ mean businesses can for improvement. It’s a careful strategy, allowing companies to experiment without betting the farm. By choosing incremental changes, firms can address immediate needs while collecting data on what works best. It’s a way to boost efficiency and maintain flexibility, crucial in ever-changing market conditions.\ For instance, retail companies are using GenAI to refine customer service interactions rather than creating entirely new systems. With personalized responses and shopping experiences, they're enhancing satisfaction with existing technology. Similarly, enterprises are integrating GenAI into their customer product touchpoints. This integration is cautious but significant, offering innovative solutions that impact the bottom line.\ The adoption of this gradual method also has its advantages. It means fewer disruptions and less risk of failure, while also offering the opportunity to scale successful implementations. By advancing in small steps, companies can progressively lay a foundation for more extensive transformations, ensuring they keep pace with technological evolution without being overwhelmed.Integrating GenAI into Everyday Tasks is revolutionizing everyday work tasks, particularly in the realms of document generation and information synthesis. Companies are increasingly deploying AI-driven tools for these specific tasks across various departments. These are not futuristic concepts but present-day implementations that are reshaping conventional workflows.\ Document generation has become markedly more efficient with GenAI. Businesses can now produce complex documents in a fraction of the time it traditionally took, allowing employees to refocus their efforts on more strategic tasks. Take, for instance, a sales team drafting tailored proposals. GenAI rapidly assembles these documents with precision, significantly reducing the time required while enhancing the quality and personalization of content.\ Information synthesis, another powerful application, transforms how businesses deal with vast amounts of data. GenAI tools scan, aggregate, and present data interpretively, making information readily accessible and actionable. This capability streamlines processes such as research and reporting, facilitating quick decision-making and positioning businesses to be more responsive to market changes. Crucially, secure and customized integrations with office productivity suites ensure that these AI solutions operate seamlessly within existing ecosystems. Privacy concerns are managed through encrypted and isolated AI environments, maintaining the confidentiality and integrity of business information. By embedding GenAI into everyday tasks, businesses don’t just keep pace—they innovate, saving valuable time and delivering superior outcomes that were previously unattainable.Role-Specific GenAI ApplicationsIn sectors that rely heavily on specialized knowledge, the use of GenAI is becoming transformative. Coders and data scientists, for example, are leveraging tailored GenAI tools to refine their approaches and outputs. These tools assist with debugging code, suggesting improvements, and even auto-completing complex algorithms. By cutting hours of manual effort, they allow developers to focus on innovative problem-solving aspects, improving the overall software development process.\ In the realm of content creation, journalists and marketers are using GenAI to draft initial copies or generate ideas from data sets. This not only speeds up production but also provides fresh angles that a human mind might overlook. Yet, the importance of keeping a 'human in the loop' cannot be overstated. In these specialized roles, GenAI acts as a collaborator rather than a replacement. Human oversight ensures that while AI tools suggest valuable inputs, the nuanced understanding and judgment of a human refine and contextualize the output.\ This approach is not limited to coding or writing. In fields like finance, AI-powered tools analyze spreadsheets and financial models, spotting trends and anomalies with precision. However, human expertise remains crucial to interpret and apply these findings. This balance underscores the principle of GenAI serving as an enhancement, ensuring that technology aids rather than disrupts the professional landscape.Enhancing Customer-Facing Interactions with GenAI is reshaping customer service and personalized shopping, offering businesses new ways to engage and satisfy their clients. By integrating and personalized recommendations, companies aim to streamline customer interactions, making them more efficient and effective. Starbucks, for example, has integrated GenAI into its mobile app, enabling personalized suggestions based on purchasing history and preferences. The AI system recommends drinks or food items, providing tailor-made choices that encourage customers to explore and enjoy a broader range of offerings.\ At Coach, AI takes personalization further by enhancing the shopping experience both online and in-store. Through GenAI algorithms, customers receive recommendations for accessories and outfits tailored to their style preferences. Shoppers browsing online are gently nudged towards items curated by AI, which considers previous purchases and wish list items. In-store, sales staff are equipped with AI insights to better serve customers, knowing their likes and potential interests with precision.\ These applications don't just enhance customer offerings—. AI not only learns from each interaction but adapts, ensuring each experience is better than the last. Ultimately, the strategic deployment of GenAI in these sectors demonstrates the potential for innovation without losing the essential human touch, creating a seamless blend of technology and personalization.Navigating Challenges in GenAI AdoptionData security, regulatory concerns, and scalability are critical hurdles businesses face in adopting GenAI. Securing sensitive data in AI systems is paramount. Stories of data breaches have made organizations wary, and protecting customer data is crucial. Companies can navigate this by implementing robust encryption protocols and securing private AI models, ensuring data remains confidential.\ Regulatory concerns also play a significant role in stymying GenAI adoption. Varying laws and standards across regions complicate compliance. For instance, GDPR in Europe mandates strict guidelines on data privacy. Businesses must stay abreast of regional regulations and work closely with legal teams to develop compliant AI tools. This might involve detailed audits and rigorous training for their teams to ensure proper handling of customer data.\ Scalability presents another challenge. Pilot projects run smoothly, but scaling them can be fraught with technical challenges and resource constraints. Adopting a phased approach is crucial. Companies like Amazon have shown success by incrementally expanding AI capabilities, starting with manageable deployments before scaling up.\ Moreover, establishing clear metrics helps track progress and refine strategies, ensuring the systems grow efficiently and effectively. Addressing these challenges head-on can transform barriers into gateways, allowing businesses to fully exploit GenAI's potential, thereby fostering innovation and operational excellence.Leadership’s Role in GenAI IntegrationLeadership is the linchpin in the successful integration of generative AI into business operations. For businesses to venture into GenAI adoption, leaders must not only understand the technology but also strategically shape its role within their companies. Adept leadership means acknowledging the potential of GenAI while aligning it with organizational goals and policies. It begins with a commitment to invest wisely in both data management and AI governance frameworks—key elements in harnessing GenAI's power responsibly and effectively.\ A critical step for leaders is fostering a culture that is both curious and cautious about AI. By promoting education and training, leaders can demystify GenAI, making it accessible to employees across levels. Leaders also need to ensure robust data governance, establishing clear protocols that protect privacy and comply with regulations. This clarity allows GenAI to be used confidently, mitigating risks associated with data misuse.\ Simultaneously, leadership must advocate for transparency and accountability in AI use. By setting clear guidelines and ethical considerations, leaders create an environment where innovation thrives without compromising integrity. These strategic efforts, grounded in a deep understanding of both AI possibilities and business needs, position companies to benefit from GenAI's advancements while paving the way for sustainable, transformative growth in the future.Looking ahead, the strategic integration of GenAI provides businesses with a unique opportunity to reshape the landscape of innovation. By investing in cross-cutting capabilities, companies position themselves not just for incremental gains, but for fundamental transformations over time. Imagine a world where AI-driven insights aren't confined to single tasks or roles, but seamlessly influence entire business models and industry standards. This future isn't far off.\ Corporations like Google and Amazon are pioneering large-scale GenAI integrations, demonstrating how AI can redefine supply chains, drive unprecedented personalization, and inspire new market dynamics. For instance, AI-powered logistics can anticipate demand, while platforms like Netflix use sophisticated algorithms to craft personalized viewing experiences.\ But the path to such wide-reaching transformation requires patience and foresight. Businesses need foundational investments in infrastructure and data stewardship. Security, ethical AI practices, and robust data management are essential. These investments ensure scalable and reliable AI systems that fuel long-term growth.\ Moreover, companies need to cultivate a culture of experimentation and learning. Encouraging teams to explore GenAI’s potential spurs creativity and uncovers hidden opportunities. Cross-departmental teams should collaboratively build AI solutions, gaining insights and iterating rapidly. Organizations must remain nimble, ready to pivot as GenAI technologies evolve. Looking ahead, embracing GenAI’s expansive potential is not just about keeping pace—it's about setting the pace.

item.title: The Age of Marketing Funnels is Over
item.author: James Effarah
item.content:encoded: Out With Funnels, In With Gravity\ For years, B2B SaaS marketers have clung to the funnel like a lifeboat: awareness, consideration, decision. Wash, rinse, repeat.\ But today’s buying journeys aren’t linear, and the funnel feels as outdated as a dial-up modem.\ Enter —a new way to think about customer acquisition.\ It’s less about shoving leads through predetermined stages and more about building a gravitational pull so strong that customers are drawn in on their own.\ Think omnipresent content, strategic influence, and an ecosystem where your brand becomes the obvious choice.“Digital gravity isn’t about chasing customers. It’s about being the center of their universe when they’re ready to make a move.”Funnels worked when buyers followed predictable paths. But in 2025, that’s a fantasy.“Modern purchasing decisions are anything but linear. They’re messy, multi-dimensional, and influenced by everything from peer reviews to random LinkedIn posts.”\ And the traditional funnel assumes all leads are equal, ignoring the nuances of behavior, intent, and context.\ A clunky model that oversimplifies complex buying decisions—especially in the B2B SaaS world.If funnels push, gravity pulls.\ The premise is simple: become unavoidable.\ By building —content, trust, and presence.\ Everywhere your audience hangs out.\ Blog posts, videos, podcasts, social media—you name it.\ But it’s not just about throwing spaghetti at the wall.\ Quality and relevance are everything.“If your content doesn’t provide value, it’s just noise. You need to create something your audience actually cares about.”\ To be top of mind, not in their face.\ Digital gravity lets potential customers orbit your brand, entering your ecosystem when they’re ready, not when you’re desperate.Bootstrapped vs. Funded: Building Gravity on a BudgetStartups often face a tough question: build momentum now or conserve resources?\ Funded companies can afford to (AND SHOULD) play the field, pumping out content across multiple platforms.\ Bootstrapped startups, on the other hand, need to focus their energy.\ Don’t try to be everywhere.\ Find the channels that matter most to your audience and own them.Tactical Moves to Harness Digital Gravity\ How do you build this elusive pull?Scrape Smart, Target SmarterFind the Right People: Use tools like Apollo.io to zero in on industries, job titles, and company sizes that fit your product.Gather Intel: Tools like Hunter.io can help you collect emails—ethically, of course. (Privacy laws exist for a reason.Laser-Focused Ads: Stop wasting money on broad campaigns. Hyper-targeted ads on LinkedIn and Google work better every time.Content That Actually WorksLeverage UGC: User-generated content (think reviews, testimonials) adds authenticity.Experiment with AI: AI avatars and personalized ads are no longer science fiction. They’re here, and they’re effective.Test Relentlessly: Run A/B tests until you figure out what sticks. Then do more of that.Cold Emails That Don’t SuckBe Personal: Research your prospects. Know their pain points and craft emails they actually want to open.Stand Out: Generic subject lines won’t cut it. Ask a bold question or highlight a specific value.Drive Action: Clear, concise, and actionable. Your email isn’t a novel; it’s an invitation.Go Multi-Channel: Ads, podcasts, blogs, emails—each should reinforce the other.Build Trust: Share thought leadership, host webinars, and publish articles that position you as an industry authority.Here’s where it gets really interesting.\ Digital gravity isn’t just about attracting customers.\ It’s also a way to make money.\ Turn your marketing into media.Ads on Your Platforms: Got traffic? Monetize it with relevant ads.Partnerships: Collaborate with brands or influencers to co-create content.Subscriptions: Offer premium content for a fee.“Marketing doesn’t have to be a cost center. Done right, it can drive revenue.”\ Think snowball effect: the more presence you have, the more people notice, engage, and convert.\ Exponential growth is about momentum.“Once you’ve built enough digital mass, the system starts working for you.”The Future of SaaS MarketingThe age of funnels is over.\ It’s a paradigm shift—one that rewards creativity, strategy, and relentless focus on the customer.\ The tools are already in your hands.\ Building digital gravity takes time, effort, and a willingness to experiment.\ But if you’re ready to break free from the funnel, the rewards are waiting.\ Welcome to the new frontier.

item.title: BigQuery and Attribution Models Can Reveal What Really Drives E-Commerce Success
item.author: Azize Sultan Palali
item.content:encoded:
\ Attribution models play a critical role in the digital marketing and ecommerce world by helping us understand user journeys and make data driven decisions. In this article, we will dive into the definitions, use cases, and implementation of 7 different attribution models using BigQuery. 🚀\ I work at a leading e-commerce company that has over 3 million distinct users daily. With such a vast user base, the platform consists of numerous components and pages, including banners, recommendation widgets, flash deals, and search results. Each of these elements plays a unique role in guiding users along their journey toward a purchase. Managing and evaluating the effectiveness of these components at scale is a significant challnge, but it is crucial for optimizing user experiences and driving business growth.\ In digital marketing, the customer journey consists of multiple touchpoints, each contributing differently to the conversion process. Accurately evaluating these touchpoints is key to making more efficient use of marketing budgets. Attribution models are the methods used to carry out this evaluation, and each model provides a unique perspective on user behavior.Why Attribution Models Are Significantly Better?The following visual summarizes the impact of different components (e.g., "Swimlane," "Horizontal Banner," "Deal of the Day") on the purchase journeys of four different users in an e-commerce platform. The visual analyzes each component in terms of views, clicks, and orders, providing valuable insights into their role at various stages of the customer journey.\ By understanding these metrics in the context of millions of daily users and hundreds of components, businesses can evaluate which elements capture user attention, drive engagement, and ultimately lead to conversions. This makes attribution models an indispensable tool for data driven decision making at scale.What Advantages Do These Models Provide?Enhancing Customer ExperienceIdentifying the correct sources allows us to gain a deeper understanding of customer behaviors and preferences. This insight enables us to deliver more targeted and personalized services, creating a more engaging and satisfying user experience.Prioritizing In App Feature DevelopmentAttribution data reveals which features are effective and which areas require improvement. For example, if we notice that users coming from a specific page have higher purchase rates, we can focus on optimizing the design or placement of that page to maximize its potential.Reporting and TransparencyAccurately reporting the performance of each source empowers teams to make more informed, data-driven decisions. Additionally, when all teams work with the same data, collaboration and alignment across departments improve significantly.Standardization and ConsistencyUsing a single attribution model for all features ensures that the data is more consistent and reliable. This standardization fosters seamless collaboration across different teams and provides a unified understanding of performance metrics.A/B Testing and ExperimentationAttribution models play a crucial role in accurately measuring the impact of A/B tests. They help determine which variation is more effective in driving conversions. Moreover, they reveal shifts in the share of orders across sources, even when overall transactional metrics remain unchanged, providing deeper insights into test outcomes.Attribution Modelleri ve MantıklarıWhich User Behavior Should We Focus On? 🧐\ Users with only a single interaction (highlighted in ) will give the same result across all attribution models, so they’re not our priority. Instead, we’ll focus on users with but only one .\ Our goal is to figure out which component had the biggest influence on their purchase. This way, we can uncover more meaningful insights and make smarter optimizations.\ The White Colored User’s Journey 👩🏻‍💻The user highlighted in has interacted with and clicked on in the following order:\ On the same day, this user made a single . Using various attribution models, we’ll identify which component triggered the order and how the credit should be distributed among these interactions. This will give us a clearer understanding of the components’ influence on the user’s decision.1. First Touch Model (Interaction, Visit, Campaign etc.)This model assigns all credit to the in the user’s journey and is commonly used to measure the effectiveness of acquisition campaigns. Its key benefit lies in identifying the channel that first introduced the user to the brand. This makes it particularly valuable for strategies focused on acquiring new customers.SELECT user_id, MIN(timestamp) AS first_touch_time, FIRST_VALUE(component) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY timestamp) AS first_touch_component FROM `project.dataset.user_journey` GROUP BY ALL; | User Info | First Interation | Credit | |----|----|----| | White Colored User | Brand Widget | 100% |The indicates the channel where the conversion journey began, providing insights into which source initially captured the user’s attention.2. Last Touch Model (Interaction, Visit, Campaign etc.)This model assigns all credit to the in the user’s journey, emphasizing the importance of the interaction closest to the conversion. Its main advantage is enabling the analysis of the component that had the most direct impact on driving the conversion. This makes it especially useful for .SELECT user_id, MAX(timestamp) AS last_touch_time, LAST_VALUE(component) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY timestamp ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING) AS last_touch_channel FROM `project.dataset.user_journey` GROUP BY ALL; | User Info | Last Interation | Credit | |----|----|----| | White Colored User | Reco Boxes | 100% |It highlights the component that directly influenced the user's decision to convert, providing valuable insights into the most impactful touchpoint in the journey.The linear model assigns equal credit to every touchpoint in the user journey, making it ideal for situations where all interactions are considered equally important. Its key advantage is treating all component fairly, acknowledging their equal contribution to the conversion. This model is particularly suitable for complex customer journeys, where multiple touchpoints play a role in influencing the user’s decision.\ With this approach, there is no distinction between the user’s and their , as every touchpoint receives the same weight in the credit distribution.SELECT user_id, channel, COUNT(component) AS touch_count, (1.0 / COUNT(component)) AS linear_credit FROM `project.dataset.user_journey` GROUP BY All; | User Info | All Interation | Credit | |----|----|----| | White Colored User | Brand Widget | 33% | | White Colored User | Deal of The Day | 33% | | White Colored User | Reco Boxes | 33% |It ensures that every interaction in the user journey is valued equally, providing a balanced view of all touchpoints' impact.The time decay model assigns decreasing weights to touchpoints as time progresses, giving more importance to interactions closer to the conversion. This model is particularly effective in scenarios involving quick decision making processes, where recent touchpoints are more influential.\ Its key benefit is the ability to better analyze the impact of recent component/page efforts, highlighting their critical role in driving conversions.WITH weighted_data AS ( SELECT user_id, component, TIMESTAMP_DIFF(MAX(timestamp), timestamp, SECOND) AS time_diff, EXP(-time_diff / 86400) AS time_decay_weight FROM `project.dataset.user_journey` GROUP BY ALL ) SELECT user_id, component, SUM(time_decay_weight) AS total_weight FROM weighted_data GROUP BY All; | User Info | All Interation | Credit | |----|----|----| | White Colored User | Brand Widget | 20% | | White Colored User | Deal of The Day | 30% | | White Colored User | Reco Boxes | 50% |It highlights the importance of recent interactions, providing a dynamic view of how proximity to the conversion impacts the effectiveness of each touchpoint.This model gives greater weight to the and , while assigning less credit to the interactions in between. This model emphasizes the importance of the components that initially introduced the user to the brand and those that finalized the conversion.\ Intermediate touchpoints, while still contributing, are considered less impactful as they primarily keep the user engaged throughout the journey.WITH touch_points AS ( SELECT user_id, component, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY timestamp) AS touch_order, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id) AS total_touches FROM `project.dataset.user_journey` ) SELECT user_id, channel, CASE WHEN touch_order = 1 THEN 0.4 WHEN touch_order = total_touches THEN 0.4 ELSE 0.2 / (total_touches - 2) END AS u_shape_credit FROM touch_points; | User Info | All Interation | Credit | |----|----|----| | White Colored User | Brand Widget | 40% | | White Colored User | Deal of The Day | 20% | | White Colored User | Reco Boxes | 40% |Highlights the most critical moments in the user journey (beginning and end).Optimizes coordination between components by identifying key contributors.Provides clear insights, especially for complex customer journeys.This model assigns greater credit to the , , and the most critical intermediate touchpoint right before the conversion. This model emphasizes multiple key moments in the user journey, providing a more balanced and comprehensive analysis of the interactions that drive conversions.WITH touch_points AS ( SELECT user_id, component, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY timestamp) AS touch_order, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id) AS total_touches FROM `project.dataset.user_journey` ) SELECT user_id, component, CASE WHEN touch_order = 1 THEN 0.3 WHEN touch_order = total_touches THEN 0.3 WHEN touch_order = ROUND(total_touches / 2) THEN 0.3 ELSE 0.1 / (total_touches - 3) END AS w_shape_credit FROM touch_points; | User Info | All Interation | Credit | |----|----|----| | White Colored User | Brand Widget | 40% | | White Colored User | Deal of The Day | 20% | | White Colored User | Reco Boxes | 40% |Evaluates not only the first and last touchpoints but also other critical moments in the conversion journey.Ideal for identifying key interactions that play a significant role in driving conversions.The Key Difference Between U-Shape and W-Shape Models\ The primary distinction between the U-Shape and W-Shape models lies in the weight assigned to intermediate components.\ While the U-Shape model distributes credit among all interactions between the first and last touchpoints, the W-Shape model assigns to intermediate interactions that are closer to the final touchpoint.\ You can compare these two models using the example below to better understand how credit distribution differs in scenarios with multiple touchpoints.The data driven model leverages machine learning and data-driven algorithms to optimize credit allocation. By analyzing user behavior, this model learns the impact of each intraction on conversions, providing a dynamic and highly accurate understanding of touchpoint contributions.CREATE MODEL `project.dataset.attribution_model`OPTIONS(model_type='logistic_reg') AS SELECT * FROM `project.dataset.user_journey_training_data`;
SELECT user_id, predicted_attribution FROM ML.PREDICT(MODEL `project.dataset.attribution_model`, (SELECT * FROM `project.dataset.user_journey_test_data`)); | User Info | All Interation | Credit | |----|----|----| | White Colored User | Brand Widget | 10% | | White Colored User | Deal of The Day | 30% | | White Colored User | Reco Boxes | 60% |Provides dynamic and personalized analysis tailored to user behavior.Well-suited for working with large datasets, ensuring accurate insights at scale.Working on our order attribution model has been an amazing experience at our company. It’s helped us understand how users interact with our platform and what drives their decisions. We’ve seen big benefits, from better team decisions to more useful insights.\ If you haven’t tried attribution modeling yet, I highly recommend it. Start small, focus on key touchpoints, and let the data show you the way. For us, it’s been a game changer, and we’re excited to keep improving it to make the user experience even better.Thank you for your time; sharing is caring! 🌍

item.title: Latest Bun Update Brings Performance Improvements
item.author: This Week in JavaScript
item.content:encoded: Hello JavaScript Enthusiasts!\ Welcome to another edition of ""!\ From Bun 1.2’s performance improvements to AI-powered web extensions in Mozilla Firefox, along with the latest tools shaking up the ecosystem, we have a lot to unpack. \n \n Let’s get right into it!Bun 1.2: Better, Faster, Stronger is making headlines in the JavaScript runtime scene, pushing the boundaries of Node.js compatibility with significant updates. Thousands of bug fixes and performance improvements make Bun a more reliable option for production-ready environments. Migrating from Node.js has never been easier, as Bun minimizes breakage and maintains functionality. Bun transitions to a text-based lockfile for easier dependency management and debugging, improving clarity in project workflows.Native Support for S3 & Postgres: Developers can now enjoy seamless integration with Amazon S3 and Postgres, accelerating cloud development setups. JSON files with comments make configuration more readable and manageable. A massive performance boost, making Express.js apps run up to 3x faster. continues to advance the landscape, delivering cutting-edge NLP (natural language processing) and TTS (text-to-speech) directly to your browser. No server-side processing is required—everything runs smoothly client-side. A near-human text-to-speech model that works within the browser, perfect for voice assistants or speech features.: Zero-shot object detection that works without labeled data, ideal for real-time applications.React Integration: Improved integration with React makes using ML models in web apps easier than ever. Test real-time TTS capabilities with impressive results, rivaling cloud services.Mozilla’s ML-Powered Web Extensions’s Nightly browser introduces a , enabling with no need for cloud processing. This privacy-first approach reduces latency while ensuring data security.\ What You Can Do with Firefox’s ML API:On-device Text Summarization: Summarize articles and web content without external servers.Object Detection & Image Classification: Perform tasks like object recognition directly in the browser.Powered by Transformers.js & ONNX Engine: Seamless integration with these tools offers fast, secure ML operations.\ This API brings privacy-focused, on-device ML to developers, creating new opportunities for AI-powered web applications.Let’s take a quick look at the latest tools making waves in the JavaScript world this week! Boosts bundling efficiency with a persistent cache (hot starts 250% faster!), Yarn PnP support, and Angular integration. Fully supports Android 15 and iOS 18, Swift Package Manager, and streamlined dependency management for cross-platform app development. Enhances Vite-native testing with multi-browser setups, line-based test filtering, and community-driven innovations. Runtime validation redefined—100x faster than Zod, bridging TypeScript to JavaScript effortlessly. Now supports React 19, React Router 7, and MUI 6, with bulk actions, string array editing, and better caching for B2B frontends.\ If you found these JavaScript updates useful, give this post an upvote to help others discover it.\ Don’t forget to share it with your fellow developers, and keep an eye out for more insights in the JavaScript world.\ Until next time, happy coding!

item.title: HomePod With Screen 'Most Significant New Apple Product' of 2025, Says Gurman
item.author: BeauHD
item.content:encoded:
In his latest Power On! newsletter, Apple analyst Mark Gurman called the company's new smart device "Apple's most significant release of the year because it's the first step toward a bigger role in the smart home." The device in question is rumored to be a new smart hub that could look like a HomePod with a seven-inch screen. Digital Trends reports: Gurman calls the new smart device a "smaller and cheaper iPad that lets users control appliances, conduct FaceTime chats and handle other tasks." It doesn't sound like the new hub will stand alone, though; Gurman goes on to say that it "should be followed by a higher-end version in a few years." That version should be able to pan and tilt to keep users in-frame during video calls, or just to keep the display visible as someone moves around the home.
[...] Other details are still known, like whether the device will use an original operating system. The overall plan is to make the new smart device the center of an Apple-based smart home and open the doors to a more conversational Siri.
